meta.title: Show Version Information | ProxMenux Documentation
meta.description: Read-only ProxMenux self-diagnostic: version number, install type (Translation / Normal), installed components from config.json, file paths, virtual environment state and current language.
meta.ogDescription: Diagnostic dump of ProxMenux version, install type, components, files and language.

header.title: Show Version Information
header.description: Read-only diagnostic. Reads version.txt, config.json and a few well-known file paths, then renders a scrollable text box with version, install type, installed components, file presence, virtual environment state and current language.

intro.title: What this is
intro.body: A self-diagnostic. Useful when reporting an issue (paste the dialog contents into the bug report) or to confirm an install completed cleanly. Modifies nothing.

rows.contentRich: Single-line version number; Unknown if file missing

rows.content: Per-component status (✓ installed / created / upgraded, ✗ missing)
Translation install only — venv + pip presence

troubleshoot.heading: Troubleshooting
troubleshoot.unknownBody: /usr/local/share/proxmenux/version.txt is missing or unreadable. Most often caused by a partial install. Reinstall from the official ProxMenux source.
troubleshoot.noConfigBody: config.json is missing. The script can't enumerate components without it. Reinstall to regenerate.
troubleshoot.wrongStatusTitle: A component shows ✗ that I'm sure I installed
troubleshoot.wrongStatusBody: Means config.json doesn't track that component as installed — could be a script that doesn't register itself yet, or a manual install that bypassed ProxMenux. The dialog reflects what config.json says, not what's actually on disk.
